questions.stem: Which process group formally authorizes a project or project phase to begin?
questions.explanation: Initiating authorizes the project or phase to start. This is typically where the project charter is created and approved.

questions.stem: What is the primary purpose of the Planning process group?
choices: Define objectives and determine how the project will achieve them
questions.explanation: Planning defines how the project will be executed and controlled. It includes defining scope, schedule, cost, risk, resources, and communication plans.

questions.stem: Which process group focuses on performing the work defined in the project management plan?
questions.explanation: Executing is where the team performs the work described in the plan to produce project deliverables.

questions.stem: In which process group are performance measurements compared against the plan?
questions.explanation: Monitoring and Controlling tracks project performance and compares it to the project management plan.

questions.stem: Which process group ensures that the project or phase is formally completed?
questions.explanation: Closing includes final acceptance of deliverables, documentation archiving, and lessons learned.

questions.stem: When does the Planning process group typically occur?
choices: Only once at the start of the project
Throughout the project whenever changes require plan updates
Only at the end of the project
questions.explanation: Planning happens initially but also continues throughout the project as updates are required.

questions.stem: Which process group is responsible for managing stakeholder engagement and directing project work?
questions.explanation: Executing includes directing and managing project work and engaging stakeholders.

questions.stem: A project manager identifies that the project schedule is slipping. Which process group primarily addresses this situation?
questions.explanation: Monitoring and Controlling identifies variances from the plan and determines corrective actions.

questions.stem: Which process group produces the project management plan?
questions.explanation: The Planning process group creates the project management plan and its subsidiary plans.

questions.stem: Who is typically assigned and formally recognized during the Initiating process group?
questions.explanation: The project charter usually identifies and authorizes the project manager.

questions.stem: Which process group focuses most on producing project deliverables?
questions.explanation: Executing is where the majority of the project work and deliverables are produced.

questions.stem: When a change request is submitted, which process group evaluates its impact on the project?
questions.explanation: Monitoring and Controlling evaluates changes and determines their impact before approval.

questions.stem: Which process group is responsible for identifying stakeholders?
questions.explanation: Stakeholders are initially identified during the Initiating process group.

questions.stem: Which process group ensures lessons learned are documented and archived?
questions.explanation: Lessons learned documentation and archiving occur during Closing.

questions.stem: A project team is creating the Work Breakdown Structure. Which process group does this belong to?
questions.explanation: The WBS is created during Planning as part of defining project scope.

questions.stem: If the project manager discovers a variance in cost performance, what should typically happen next?
choices: Analyze the variance and determine corrective action
questions.explanation: Cost variances are analyzed in Monitoring and Controlling to determine corrective actions.

questions.stem: Which process group ensures deliverables meet requirements before being accepted?
questions.explanation: Quality control and validation occur in Monitoring and Controlling.

questions.stem: Which process group focuses on coordinating people and resources to carry out the project plan?
questions.explanation: Executing involves coordinating people, resources, and activities to produce deliverables.

questions.stem: Why do projects move through all five process groups?
choices: To ensure work is planned, executed, controlled, and formally completed
To ensure the project manager has authority
questions.explanation: The process groups ensure that projects are properly authorized, planned, executed, controlled, and closed.

questions.stem: Which process group is most focused on defining how success will be measured?
questions.explanation: Planning defines success metrics, scope, schedule, and cost baselines.
